findings.evidence: `rg` shows this PR adds only `theorem fourierIntegral_one_eq_charFun_neg_two_pi_smul` at line 51; Mathlib already has `charFun_zero`, `norm_charFun_le`, and `continuous_charFun`, and TauCeti has `charFun_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg`, but no Fourier-convention wrappers are exposed in this file.
findings.fix: Mark the conversion theorem as the normal-form rewrite, e.g. `@[simp]`, and add named consumer lemmas for value at zero, norm bound, continuity of `fun a => VectorFourier.fourierIntegral Real.fourierChar μ (innerₗ E) (1 : E → ℂ) a`, and positive-definiteness via the existing `charFun` positive-definite/pullback API.
findings.issue: The only public API is an unannotated conversion theorem; the roadmap asks for the finite-measure Fourier transform as a continuous positive-definite bridge, and consumers currently get no named zero/norm/continuity/positive-definiteness lemmas for the Fourier-convention expression.

summary: The exposed bridge is too thin for the roadmap target: it gives the conversion equality, but not the annotated normal form or the continuity/positive-definiteness consumer API needed to use the Fourier-convention transform without reproving the bridge each time.
